destinations.evidence: Alloy converts signed async commitment/settlement envelopes into pooled envelopes without changing the variant.
The pooled conversion used by the port delegates to the same same-variant async conversion.
destinations.snippet: fn try_from(value: EthereumTxEnvelope<TxEip4844>) -> Result<Self, Self::Error> { value.try_into_pooled() }

destinations.evidence: The port aliases signed and pooled transaction primitives to the Alloy envelope types.
destinations.snippet:
pub type TransactionSigned = alloy_consensus::EthereumTxEnvelope<TxEip4844>;
pub type PooledTransactionVariant = alloy_consensus::EthereumTxEnvelope<TxEip4844WithSidecar<BlobTransactionSidecarVariant>>;

destinations.evidence: The pool conversion path invokes Alloy TryFrom and preserves the recovered signer.

equivalence_argument: AsyncExec is renamed/currently represented by Alloy AsyncCommitment/AsyncSettlement. The dependency-owned conversion maps async variants to same-kind pooled variants, and the port consumes that through TransactionSigned/PooledTransactionVariant aliases.

related_but_insufficient_code: Exact old AsyncExec symbols are absent; current async-exec equivalents are dependency-owned Alloy AsyncCommitment/AsyncSettlement types.

notes: Mapped in dependency with port integration evidence.
rationale: The candidate directly identifies a current tip-state conversion invariant: AsyncExec transactions must remain AsyncExec when entering the pool. The bounded neighbors show related scheduled transaction conversion and pool behavior, but none supersede or broaden this AsyncExec-specific requirement.
statement: TryFrom<TransactionSigned> for PooledTransaction must map Transaction::AsyncExec to PooledTransaction::AsyncExec, preserving the AsyncExec transaction kind during pool conversion rather than converting it to Scheduled or any other pooled variant.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct a TransactionSigned containing Transaction::AsyncExec, convert it with PooledTransaction::try_from, and assert the result matches PooledTransaction::AsyncExec with the original async-exec transaction, signature, and hash.
Add a regression case that would fail if the AsyncExec match arm returned PooledTransaction::Scheduled or UnsupportedForP2P.
summary: AsyncExec conversion is preserved by the renamed Alloy async envelope conversion consumed by the port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Construct a signed TxAsyncCommitment, convert through PooledTransactionVariant::try_from or EthPooledTransaction::try_from_consensus, and assert the result remains AsyncCommitment.
title: AsyncExec signed transactions convert to pooled AsyncExec
